name: add-endpoint
description: Add new HTTP endpoints to Catalyst-Relay server. Use when creating routes, API endpoints, or HTTP handlers.
capabilities: Create route files with colocated Zod schemas and response types
Implement handler functions using Hono framework factory pattern
Wire routes to the Hono app in the routes index
Generate API documentation with request and response examples
Map HTTP endpoints to library methods on ADTClient
author: Artisan-Edge
securitySummary: This is a documentation-only skill containing a SKILL.md file with guidance for generating API endpoint code. No executable code, no file system access beyond reading its own file, no network calls, and no command execution capabilities. Pure prompt-based skill with zero attack surface. All 38 static findings are false positives caused by the analyzer misinterpreting documentation patterns (code examples as Ruby execution, Zod schemas as crypto, template placeholders as path traversal).
valueStatement: Need to add new API routes to your Catalyst-Relay server but unsure of the proper pattern? This skill provides the complete template for creating endpoints with Zod validation, consistent response formats, and proper documentation.
userTitle: Add HTTP endpoints to Catalyst-Relay server
limitations: Cannot connect to SAP ADT servers directly
Does not validate generated code compiles

useCases.title: Add new API endpoints
useCases.description: Create authenticated REST endpoints for Catalyst-Relay with proper validation and error handling

useCases.title: Extend server functionality
useCases.description: Add new routes to expose Catalyst-Relay core functions via HTTP API

useCases.title: Build ADT API wrappers
useCases.description: Wrap SAP ADT operations with custom HTTP endpoints for frontend integration

promptTemplates.title: Create simple endpoint
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a new endpoint at src/server/routes/health/check.ts that returns server status with uptime and memory usage

promptTemplates.title: Add POST endpoint
promptTemplates.prompt: Add a POST endpoint at src/server/routes/users/search.ts that accepts search filters and returns matching user records

promptTemplates.title: Create CRUD operations
promptTemplates.prompt: Create full CRUD endpoints for managing transport requests at src/server/routes/transports/ with create, read, update, and delete operations

promptTemplates.title: Wire route to app
promptTemplates.prompt: Register the new /transports/create endpoint in src/server/routes/index.ts with session authentication middleware

bestPractices: Use the factory pattern: export a handler function that accepts dependencies
Place request schema and response types in the same file for co-location
Use as const for success and false literals to preserve discriminated union types
antiPatterns: Avoid putting multiple handlers in a single file
Do not skip Zod schema validation for request bodies
Never hardcode error codes - use the error envelope pattern

faq.question: Where should new route files be created?
faq.answer: Create route files in src/server/routes/{category}/{endpoint}.ts, e.g., auth/login.ts or preview/data.ts

faq.question: What validation library is used?
faq.answer: Zod is used for request validation with safeParse() for type-safe parsing and error handling

faq.question: How are routes registered?
faq.answer: Routes are registered in src/server/routes/index.ts using app.post(), app.get(), etc. with the handler factory

faq.question: Is authentication required for endpoints?
faq.answer: Endpoints receive sessionManager via the factory pattern. Auth requirements depend on route configuration

faq.question: What response format is required?
faq.answer: Use { success: true|false as const, data?: result, error?: message, code?: string } envelope with HTTP status codes

faq.question: How does this differ from Express or Fastify?
faq.answer: Catalyst-Relay uses Hono, a lightweight web framework with built-in TypeScript support and middleware composition
